page: Microsoft Azure F2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the F-family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. It features 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz, supported by a 50 MB L3 cache. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 33 GB, and the virtualization layer is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark evaluations position the F2 instance in the lower performance tiers. In CPU-intensive workloads, the server scores in the bottom 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as Geekbench and PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth tests, including read-only and write operations on large uncached blocks, similarly fall into the bottom 10% category. However, the processor demonstrates slightly better relative performance in extended instruction execution and database operations, ranking in the bottom 25% for PassMark extended instructions and database tests.

The F2 instance presents a low-resource profile with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a qualitative entry-level option within the compute-focused tier. Given the poor performance in high-throughput tasks like Redis SET operations and static web serving, this instance is constrained to lightweight workloads. It is suitable for basic utility computing, small-scale development or testing environments, and low-demand background tasks that do not require high memory capacity or intensive parallel processing.
description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an entry-level x86_64 server instance within the F-family, utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it provides 2 physical cores, 2 vCPUs at 2.3 GHz, and 4.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The hardware configuration includes 33 GB of local storage and lacks GPU acceleration. Across synthetic benchmarks, the F2 consistently ranks in the bottom 10% for CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though it reaches the bottom 25% in database operations and extended instruction tests. Given these resource constraints and performance metrics, the instance is best suited for lightweight utility workloads, small-scale development environments, and low-concurrency background processing rather than high-throughput database or web serving applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 33 GB local storage. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 CPU, it is designed for entry-level, lightweight utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 33 GB local storage, suitable for lightweight utility workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, compute-oriented x86_64 instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a compact memory footprint for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
4.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
33 GB of bundled local storage on Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor
Performance profile suited for lightweight utility and development tasks
Low-tier benchmark results in CPU, memory bandwidth, and web serving tests
